title: Make plus FAB on Hub page open PostTaskModal

requirements.summary: Wire the floating action button (FAB) in the bottom-right corner of HubView to open PostTaskModal for authenticated users with profiles, or trigger the existing auth/profile prompt flow if conditions aren't met
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the FAB on HubView opens PostTaskModal for a logged-in user with a profile.
Clicking the FAB when the user is not authenticated shows the existing authentication prompt (AuthPromptBar or equivalent flow).
Clicking the FAB when the user is authenticated but has no profile shows the existing profile-creation prompt flow.
After a task is successfully submitted via the modal, the task list on HubView refreshes to include the new task.
The FAB remains visible and accessible at the bottom-right of the page across all filter/category states.

file_operations.description: Add click handler to the existing floating action button that checks authentication and profile status (reusing the same guard logic currently present in TaskCard.tsx's handleClick), then opens PostTaskModal if authorized or triggers showAuthPrompt otherwise. Ensure the FAB remains visible and functional across all filter/category states. The modal state and refresh logic already exist in the component; wire them to the FAB onClick event.
